seoTitle: How to Enter an Owner's Draw in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Record an owner's draw in QuickBooks Online to track distributions accurately without disrupting your business's financial records.
intro: An owner's draw lets business owners take distributions while keeping a clear record of the financial impact on the company's books. This guide covers how to enter an owner's draw in QuickBooks Online, useful for sole proprietors, freelancers, and small business owners who need to separate personal withdrawals from business finances.

steps.heading: Set up an owner's draw account
steps.body: Create an equity account in QuickBooks Online specifically for tracking owner's draws if one doesn't already exist.

steps.heading: Record the draw transaction
steps.body: Enter the withdrawal as a transaction against the owner's draw account, including the date and amount.

steps.heading: Categorize it correctly
steps.body: Make sure the transaction is categorized as an equity draw rather than an expense, since it isn't a business cost.

steps.heading: Review your financial records
steps.body: Check your balance sheet to confirm the draw is reflected accurately and isn't affecting your profit and loss statement incorrectly.

steps.heading: Track draws consistently
steps.body: Keep a habit of recording each draw the same way so your equity records stay clean over time.

faq.question: Is an owner's draw the same as a salary?
faq.answer: No, a draw is a distribution of equity rather than a wage, and it's recorded differently in QuickBooks than payroll.

faq.question: Does an owner's draw affect my business's taxable income?
faq.answer: Draws are generally not a deductible business expense, so recording them correctly matters for accurate tax reporting; consult a tax professional for specifics.

faq.question: Who typically uses the owner's draw feature?
faq.answer: Sole proprietors, partners, and freelancers who take distributions rather than a formal salary commonly use this feature.

faq.question: Can I automate tracking of owner's draws?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online supports integrations and recurring transaction features that can help streamline recording regular draws.
